title: Distributed Cache Service
description: Design a distributed in-memory cache service (like Redis or Memcached) with LRU eviction, consistent hashing, and replication.
requirements: GET and SET operations with optional TTL
LRU eviction when memory limit is reached
Consistent hashing for key distribution across nodes
Replication to at least one replica node per shard
Support cache-aside, write-through, and write-behind patterns
nonFunctionalReqs: < 1ms GET/SET latency P99 for in-memory hits
Survive single node failure with < 100ms failover

components.label: Client
components.javaNote: Application using the cache client library. Client library implements consistent hashing to route keys to correct node. Handles retry on node failure. Popular: Jedis (Redis), Memcached Java client, or custom.

components.label: Cache Service
components.javaNote: Core cache node. Uses a ConcurrentHashMap for O(1) lookup + doubly linked list for LRU order tracking. GET: move node to head (O(1)). SET: add to head, evict tail if capacity exceeded. All operations thread-safe with fine-grained locking (stripe by key hash).

components.label: Eviction Manager
components.javaNote: Background thread runs every 100ms. Two passes: (1) passive — check TTL on accessed keys; (2) active — sample 20 random keys, evict expired, repeat until <25% are expired. Same strategy as Redis. LRU eviction triggered synchronously on SET when memory > maxMemoryMB.

components.label: Backing Store (DB)
components.javaNote: Source of truth. Cache-aside: app reads DB on miss and populates cache. Write-through: write to cache + DB synchronously on SET. Write-behind (write-back): write to cache immediately, flush to DB asynchronously in batches. Write-behind risks data loss on crash.

components.label: Replication Node
components.javaNote: Each primary shard has 1+ replicas. Replication is asynchronous — primary acks client after writing to its own memory. Replica applies changes from replication log. On primary failure: replica promoted (leader election via Zookeeper or Raft). Reads can serve from replicas to distribute load.

componentBreakdown.heading: LRU Implementation with LinkedHashMap
componentBreakdown.detail: Java LinkedHashMap with accessOrder=true automatically maintains LRU order. Override removeEldestEntry to evict when size exceeds capacity. Wrap in Collections.synchronizedMap or use ConcurrentLinkedHashMap (Guava Cache) for thread safety.

componentBreakdown.heading: Consistent Hashing for Node Routing
componentBreakdown.detail: Consistent hashing maps keys to a ring of virtual nodes. Adding/removing a node only remaps ~1/N keys (vs 100% with modulo hashing). Each physical node has 150-300 virtual nodes on the ring for even distribution.

componentBreakdown.heading: Thundering Herd Problem
componentBreakdown.detail: When a popular cached item expires, many concurrent requests hit the DB simultaneously. Solutions: (1) Cache locking — only one request fetches, others wait. (2) Probabilistic early expiration — refresh before expiry. (3) Stale-while-revalidate — return stale data while refreshing async.
